directory_submission_profile.name: Decide Policy Notaries
directory_submission_profile.summary: Four deterministic, read-only, source-backed policy tools for subscription support: refund eligibility, cancellation penalties, returns, and trial terms. Results fail closed to UNKNOWN when facts are insufficient.

principles: One canonical four-tool listing concentrates installs, trust, usage, and maintenance.
Specialist endpoints remain compatibility surfaces, not four separate acquisition products.
Primary registries and client-integrated catalogs receive direct ownership; scraper mirrors refresh from canonical metadata.
Directory counters measure discovery activity, not customers or revenue.

directories.observed_evidence: Official Registry publication workflow completed successfully and the strict live distribution audit found version 1.3.1 with all four canonical tools.
directories.next_action: Monitor the immutable 1.3.1 record; publish a new version only for a material endpoint or tool-contract change.
directories.update_policy: Registry versions are immutable. Publish a new server.json version when canonical public metadata, tool contract, or remote endpoint changes; do not resubmit unchanged releases.

directories.next_action: Preserve for compatibility; point new discovery work to the canonical suite.
directories.update_policy: Do not attempt to replace or unpublish the immutable legacy record.

directories.observed_evidence: The refund-only page remains active with visible usage history. An authenticated metadata PATCH from the current decidefyi Smithery identity returned 403 because that identity does not own refund-decide/notary.
directories.next_action: Preserve the qualified name and usage history. Request an ownership transfer or metadata correction from Smithery before relabeling it as the refund compatibility endpoint; do not publish a duplicate.
directories.update_policy: Deploy a new Smithery release when the endpoint URL, connection configuration, or published tool contract changes.

directories.observed_evidence: Smithery external release c67357b9-9851-42ad-9f1f-4852dcce80cb points to https://policy.decide.fyi/api/mcp and exposes all four policy tools. A Smithery-managed connection listed the exact four-tool contract and returned Adobe refund verdict ALLOWED / WITHIN_WINDOW with source and signed Rulebook evidence.
directories.next_action: Monitor installs and tools/call activity on the canonical suite. Preserve the separate refund compatibility listing and update its description when Smithery metadata editing is available.
directories.update_policy: Publish a new release for endpoint or tool-contract changes; edit descriptive metadata for positioning changes.

directories.observed_evidence: Correction PR describes the canonical four-tool suite in Support and Service Management, removes the old refund-only Finance entry, includes the required live Glama score badge, and is clean and mergeable. A maintainer clarification was posted at issuecomment-5010951656 because the automated duplicate and missing-glama labels are stale.
directories.next_action: Monitor PR 1830 for maintainer review or label refresh; do not open a duplicate submission.
directories.update_policy: Update only for material product identity, endpoint, or tool-set changes.

directories.observed_evidence: The live catalog exposes Decide Policy Notaries, the canonical policy.decide.fyi MCP URL, all four policy tools, and the canonical one-click install configuration.
directories.next_action: No manual sync is needed. Monitor after material repository or canonical endpoint changes; claim ownership only if a future crawl remains stale.
directories.update_policy: Prefer repository and official-registry refreshes over manually maintaining duplicate descriptions.

directories.observed_evidence: PulseMCP's server submission path states that it ingests the Official MCP Registry daily and processes entries weekly. The canonical Policy Notaries release is less than one week old and is not yet present in PulseMCP search.
directories.next_action: Allow the weekly ingestion window to complete. If the canonical listing is still absent one week after the registry release, email hello@pulsemcp.com with the Official Registry identity.
directories.update_policy: No routine resubmission; monitor downstream registry ingestion.

directories.observed_evidence: The authenticated MCP.so remote-server submission accepted https://policy.decide.fyi/api/mcp under its no-cost queued-review option and confirmed: Submission received. It is now in the review queue. Its preview route remains non-public while queued.
directories.next_action: Normalize the queued submission against directory_submission_profile before publication, then monitor the review outcome and measure referral or install activity.
directories.update_policy: Refresh only for a material endpoint or tool-set change.

directories.observed_evidence: The live endpoint advertises four titled tools with explicit read-only, destructive, and open-world annotations plus output schemas. chatgpt-app-submission.json contains the review copy, all four hint justifications, exactly five positive tests, and exactly three negative tests.
directories.next_action: Validate the live server in ChatGPT Developer Mode, complete OpenAI organization verification and app permissions, then import the submission JSON and submit the plugin for review.
directories.update_policy: Create a new plugin version for public tool-contract or reviewed metadata changes; server-only compatible fixes do not need a new directory identity.

directories.observed_evidence: The remote connector meets the public no-auth connection shape and exposes titles plus applicable tool annotations. The source-controlled Anthropic packet includes listing, connection, use-case, data-handling, and reviewer-test copy.
directories.next_action: Use a Claude Team or Enterprise organization with directory-management access to submit the remote server through the Claude.ai admin portal.
directories.update_policy: Update the managed listing after material tool, scope, privacy, or documentation changes.

directories.observed_evidence: The repository now contains a Cursor marketplace manifest and a valid plugin bundle with the canonical remote MCP configuration, README, and policy-support skill.
directories.next_action: Publish the package in the public repository, validate the committed paths, then submit the repository URL to the Cursor Marketplace review portal.
directories.update_policy: Keep the plugin version synchronized with material MCP tool-contract or skill changes.

directories.observed_evidence: Docker MCP Registry PR 4471 adds the required server.yaml, empty tools.json for dynamic discovery, and readme.md documentation link using the canonical streamable HTTP endpoint. The YAML parses locally and the documentation URL returns HTTP 200; Docker Desktop and Task were unavailable for a local catalog import.
directories.next_action: Monitor upstream review and CI, respond to requested changes, and verify the catalog install after merge.
directories.update_policy: Update only when the remote URL, transport, identity, or discoverable tool contract changes.

directories.identity: MCPList, aibase, EnterpriseDNA, and similar mirrors
directories.next_action: Do not chase every mirror. Correct harmful claims or a high-traffic stale page only after primary metadata is canonical.
directories.update_policy: Rely on primary registry, Smithery, README, and well-known metadata refreshes.
